zh.Powys, Montgomeryshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan II Stafford於大約1344年出生在英國斯塔福爾時，她的父親Sir Ralph Stafford44歲，母親Margaret de Audley35歲。她於大約1360年與John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1397年11月12日在她的家鄉享年54歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾斯恩頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan II Stafford于大约1344年出生在英国斯塔福爾时，她的父亲Sir Ralph Stafford44岁，母亲Margaret de Audley35岁。她于大约1360年与John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1397年11月12日在她的家乡享年54岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾斯恩頓。

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan II Stafford was born about 1344, in Staffordshire, England, her father, Sir Ralph Stafford, was 44 and her mother, Margaret de Audley, was 35. She married John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis about 1360, in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 12 November 1397, in her hometown, at the age of 54, and was buried in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan II Stafford est née environ 1344, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Ralph Stafford, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Audley, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis environ 1360, à Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 novembre 1397, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat um 1360 in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 12. November 1397 im Alter von 54 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan II Stafford è nata il circa 1344 nel Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Sir Ralph Stafford, aveva 44 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Audley, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis circa 1360, nel Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 12 novembre 1397, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Swynnerton, Staffordshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan II Stafford(이)가 대략 1344년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Ralph Stafford(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Audley(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1360년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르에서 John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1397년 11월 12일 그녀의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 스윈르턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan II Stafford nasceu aproximadamente 1344, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Ralph Stafford, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Audley, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis aproximadamente 1360, em Montgomeryshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 12 de novembro de 1397, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Roales, Roales de Campos, Valladolid, Ca: роеилесь, роеилесь де кампос, валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan II Stafford родилась около 1344 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Ralph Stafford было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Audley, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis около 1360 г. в местности монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 12 ноября 1397 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности свинниртон, стаффордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan II Stafford nació aproximadamente 1344, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Ralph Stafford, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Margaret de Audley, tenía 35 años. Se casó con John de Cherleton 3rd Baron Cherleton of Powis aproximadamente 1360, en Montgomeryshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 12 de noviembre de 1397, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultada en Swynnerton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
